claim: Context rot (quality degrades as context increases) is solvable via small model preprocessing; use small models to manage context before frontier models
actionable: Add small-model preprocessing pipeline (NER, classification, filtering) before passing to frontier models to reduce context and improve quality
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked

claim: Small models for tool calling effectively reduce token usage; wrap small-model classifiers as tools for agents to manage context dynamically
actionable: Use small embedding and classification models as tool calls in agent workflows; chain small models before frontier model calls
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked

claim: Single GPU per model wastes compute; small models (Stella, Glyner) occupy only gigabytes; hot-swapping with LRU eviction maximizes GPU utilization
actionable: Implement shared GPU inference with model eviction policies; don't provision one GPU per model; enable dynamic model swapping
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked

claim: No open-source solution bridges from model inference development to production at scale; gap requires custom code for routing, auto-scaling, monitoring
actionable: Plan for custom infrastructure; use Superlinked SAI or similar for production small-model inference; don't assume off-the-shelf tools exist
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked

claim: Open-source models on Hugging Face are rapidly improving in quality; for narrow tasks, open models outperform managed services on benchmarks like MTEB
actionable: Evaluate open-source models seriously for specific tasks; don't assume closed-source APIs are always better; test on domain benchmarks
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked

claim: Different model architectures require different inference optimizations: BERT vs Qwen differ in flash attention, positional embeddings, query/key fusion capability
actionable: Don't assume universal inference engine; architect for model-specific optimization; account for attention mechanisms and positional encoding differences
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked

claim: Token-based batching causes compute waste with variable-length inputs; padding-aware variable-length flash attention eliminates waste
actionable: Use variable-length attention for batching; avoid padding shorter inputs to longest-request length; implement efficient packing algorithms
source_title: The Small Model Infrastructure Nobody Built (So We Did) — Filip Makraduli, Superlinked
